message:
fix: use guaranteed send for UiShutdown on bounded event channel

run_interactive() and quit_cmd() used try_send() to enqueue
PiMsg::UiShutdown, which silently drops the signal when the bounded
channel is full under backpressure. This leaves the async-to-UI bridge
stuck after the TUI exits.

Switch to an awaitable .send() path via new enqueue_pi_event() and
enqueue_ui_shutdown() helpers. The post-loop shutdown path in
run_interactive() now creates a fresh Cx::for_request() scope so bridge
teardown works even if the ambient interactive context is cancelled.

In quit_cmd() (keybindings.rs), replace try_send with a spawned async
task that calls enqueue_ui_shutdown(), preventing the quit path from
losing the shutdown signal when the event queue is saturated.

The extension UI event forwarding loop also switches to
enqueue_pi_event() so extension UI requests are no longer silently
dropped under backpressure -- the loop breaks cleanly on send failure
(channel closed) instead.

Test infrastructure: split runtime_handle() into runtime() +
runtime_handle(), add build_test_app_with_event_rx() for inspecting
queued messages. Add regression tests for shutdown-under-backpressure
and extension UI requests under backpressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
